title: introduce an element that is to be used anywhere where i am typing a reference to a secret. it should autocomplete as i type, showing me the closest match based on levenshtein distance. the same code should be used to also add a 'did you mean X' when himitsu search returns no results
Update command palette layout

title: add support for TUI hints - a small bit of text that can render in subtle text on the bottom left corner, sort of like a floating element
all form fields should have placeholders containing an example
add a submit button to the new secret tui, and add a confirm dialog when clicking esc that lets you save or discard
Add tags support for secrets (search + env composition)
description:
## Motivation

Today a secret's only addressable axis is its path. That works for hierarchies (`prod/api/STRIPE_KEY`) but not for crosscutting groupings — "everything PCI", "everything the mobile team owns", "rotate-this-quarter". Users currently fake it with path conventions, which collide with the env-prefix model.

Add free-form tags to secrets so that:
- An item can carry many tags (`["pci", "stripe", "mobile"]`).
- `himitsu search` and `himitsu ls` can filter by tag.
- The env DSL can select by tag, so an env like `pci-prod` can be authored as `tag: pci` instead of enumerating paths.

## Data model

Tags live on `SecretValue` (the encrypted payload), as `repeated string tags = 9` in `proto/secrets.proto`. They travel with the value, are written/read through the existing age envelope, and stay atomic with the secret they describe.

`SecretEntry.tags` (already declared at `proto/secrets.proto:89`) is repurposed as the manifest projection of the same field — populated by listing code that decrypts to surface metadata, identical to how descriptions are currently surfaced in `cli::search::search_core`.

Backwards compatibility: empty tags is the default; legacy envelopes round-trip through `crypto::secret_value::decode` unchanged.

Tag grammar: `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+`, 1–64 chars, no whitespace, case-sensitive. Validated at write time.

## Authoring surfaces

### CLI
- `himitsu set <path> <value> --tag <t> --tag <t> ...` — repeatable flag on `cli/set.rs`.
- `himitsu write <path> --tag <t>` — same flag wired through `cli/write.rs`.
- `himitsu tag <path> add <tag>...` / `rm <tag>...` / `list` — new subcommand at `rust/src/cli/tag.rs`, registered in `cli/mod.rs`. Mutates by decrypt → edit `tags` → re-encrypt → write.

### TUI
- `tui/views/new_secret.rs`: add a `Tags` step (comma-separated input, validated per char as the user types) between `Description` and `EnvKey`.
- `tui/views/secret_viewer.rs`: render tags as chips in the metadata pane.
- `tui/views/search.rs`: render tag chips next to each result row; clicking/pressing a chip refines the search.

## Search & filter

- `himitsu search <query> [--tag t]...`: AND-semantics across tag flags; empty query + tags = "all secrets with these tags". Tags read off the same `Decoded` struct produced by `search_core` decrypt loop.
- `himitsu ls --tag t [--tag t]...`: same AND filter, applied after path-prefix walk.
- Query-string sugar (out of scope for this epic, file as follow-up): `tag:foo bar` syntax inside the freeform query.

## Env DSL — new `Tag` entry

Extend `EnvEntry` (`rust/src/config/mod.rs:191`) with a `Tag(String)` variant. YAML shapes the deserializer accepts (extending `EnvEntry::deserialize` at `mod.rs:215`):

- Inline string with `tag:` prefix:
  ```yaml
  envs:
    pci-prod:
      - tag:pci
      - tag:prod
  ```
  AND-semantics across multiple `tag:` entries inside one env (consistent with CLI `--tag` repetition).

- Map form for explicit alias-rename, parallel to existing alias:
  ```yaml
  envs:
    pci-prod:
      - { tag: pci }            # all pci-tagged secrets, key = last path segment
      - { STRIPE: tag:stripe }  # alias: env-key STRIPE := the (singular) stripe-tagged secret
  ```
  Map-form must error when more than one secret matches the tag (alias requires a single value).

Resolver (`rust/src/config/env_resolver.rs:63`) gains a parallel branch to `resolve_concrete` / `resolve_wildcard`: walk `available_secrets`, decrypt-or-cache to read `tags`, include those that match. Wildcard capture refs (`$1`) are not extended to tag entries in this epic — out of scope.

## Out of scope
- Query-string `tag:` sugar inside `search <query>` (file as follow-up after this lands).
- Sharing/inheriting tags across stores via policy.
- Tag autocomplete in the TUI tag input (nice-to-have follow-up).
- Capture-ref interpolation inside `Tag` env entries.
- Renaming/merging tags as a first-class operation (do it manually via `tag rm` + `tag add`).

## Success criteria
- `himitsu set foo --tag a --tag b` round-trips: `himitsu get foo` shows tags in the metadata block, `himitsu tag foo list` prints `a\nb`.
- `himitsu search --tag a` returns only secrets carrying tag `a`; same for `ls --tag a`.
- `himitsu codegen <env>` against an env with `- tag:a` produces a SOPS bundle whose keys are exactly the secrets tagged `a`.
- TUI new-secret form accepts comma-separated tags; secret-viewer renders them; search rows show tag chips.
- All existing tests pass; new tests cover: tag validation, `EnvEntry` (de)serialization round-trip for both string and map forms, resolver behavior with tag entries, `search`/`ls` filter logic.
- Legacy secrets (no tags field) decode unchanged.

title: auto-detect if recipient list doesn't contain own age key
description:
## Context
 in that case, the sync status should glow orange and tell you to run 'himitsu join' which would simply add own public key to the stores recipient list, commit, and push. github actions should then rekey in a self-serve way if it is configured with a key in secrets. adding this key to secrets is also something that himitsu can do for you automatically. this task can be seen as idempotently ensuring those 3 conditions are met
notes: Scope per description: idempotently ensure (1) own pubkey is in the store's recipient list, (2) GitHub Actions has a configured age key in repo secrets, (3) auto-rekey via Actions is wired up. Surface state via TUI store-health glow + 'himitsu join' command that performs (1)-(3). Significant product/UX design — defer until owner aligns on join command shape and Actions self-serve flow.
close_reason: Implemented himitsu join command and TUI NotRecipient health indicator

title: himitsu isn't automatically running init when user has no store
notes: Investigation 2026-05-03: tui::run (rust/src/tui/mod.rs:33) auto-launches the wizard only on missing key (~/.local/share/himitsu/key). Gap: a user with a key but no resolved store gets an empty dashboard. Fix would be to also trigger run_init_flow when ctx.store.as_os_str().is_empty(). Risk: run_init_flow currently calls run() recursively after the wizard completes (mod.rs:126); init can complete-and-Success without creating a store if --name/--project are not used in the wizard, which would create an infinite loop. Either (a) require --name in the wizard's success path, (b) only re-enter run() when wizard reports a store was created, or (c) return Ok(()) from run() when store is still empty post-wizard. Needs interactive testing.
close_reason: TUI now launches init flow when key exists but no store resolves, with post-wizard no-store guard.

title: Cross-repo ref: prompt to clone missing referenced store

Non-interactive (no TTY): error with hint to run remote add. Add --yes to bypass confirmation for scripted use. TUI: small modal.

title: Cross-repo ref: prefer github:org/repo#path syntax (accept legacy / form)
description: Switch the canonical cross-repo reference syntax from github:org/repo/path/to/secret to github:org/repo#path/to/secret to disambiguate the slug from the path. Parser must accept both forms; the # form is the canonical one (used in docs, error messages, codegen). The slash form continues to parse as a fallback.

Always run the global step first (skipped if already configured), labeled 'configure global store', then run a separate 'configure project' step (skipped if not in a git repo).

title: revamp envs feature.
description:
## Context
 envs should ultimately be stored like this:
# config.yaml
...
envs:
  my-env:
   - SOME_KEY: some-exact-match # optionally override env key
 - other-exact-match # uses env key set on item or fallsback to upper(replace(name, -,_)) (forward slash  replaced with __)
 - arbitrary-glob/*
my-env-{dev,prod,stg}:
 - SOME_KEY: ref-requires-{} # requires specifying where to replace
 - some-group/item-name-{} # using the {} is not required if you have a better format


Therefore, the env editor should have an initial browser where i can select an existing env or add a new one, then take me into a 2-panel layout where I am editing the left side as if it was hx and the right pane shows me a preview of what env would be generated based on matches and/or any errors. there should be fuzzy find support and some auto-complete like feature that lets me discover the right labels as im editing

Dispatcher now commits submodules first, pushes them first, holds back parent push if any submodule push fails. auto_pull gains --recurse-submodules. Safe detached-HEAD auto-checkout only when HEAD matches origin default-branch tip.

title: himitsu codegen <env> emits <env>.sops.yaml
description:
Extend `himitsu codegen` (rust/src/cli/codegen.rs) to accept an env label and emit a valid SOPS file at `<env>.sops.yaml`. Today codegen's `--env` flag is already threaded through (see rust/src/cli/codegen.rs:55-56) for language bindings; this task adds a new output mode where the env itself is the target.

## CLI surface

- `himitsu codegen <env>` — shorthand for `--lang sops --env <env> --output <env>.sops.yaml`.
- `himitsu codegen <env> --output path.yaml` — override output path.
- Accepts concrete labels (`foo/dev`) and wildcards (`foo/*`); wildcard form emits one file per matched child (`foo-dev.sops.yaml`, `foo-prod.sops.yaml`), or a single merged file if `--merge` is given (decide in review).

## Output format

- Top-level keys come from the resolved env entries (hm-qjz), each mapped to its decrypted secret value.
- File is encrypted via `sops` rules: either the project's existing `.sops.yaml` rules file or an inline `creation_rules` block derived from himitsu recipients (TBD — call out in design doc).
- Generated file contains the same `AUTO-GENERATED` header banner codegen already emits (rust/src/cli/codegen.rs:537).

## Acceptance

- `himitsu codegen foo/dev` produces `foo-dev.sops.yaml` that `sops -d` can decrypt with the configured recipients.
- Wildcard case produces the expected file set.
- Errors clearly when the env label is unknown or when captures fail to resolve.
notes:
## Integration decision (2026-04-21)

**Shell out to the `sops` CLI.** Generate plaintext YAML from the resolved env tree, write it to the output path, then invoke `sops --encrypt --in-place <path>`. Key/recipient resolution is delegated to the user's existing `.sops.yaml` rules file.

- Requires `sops` on PATH — detect and emit a helpful error if missing.
- No new crypto crate dependency.
- Existing `AUTO-GENERATED` header banner applies to the plaintext emission before encryption.
close_reason: Landed: codegen <env> positional triggers sops path. Resolver->decrypt->YAML->sops encrypt-in-place. Missing-sops error has install hint. 6 new tests green, 1 ignored smoke.

title: Env resolver: expand wildcards and capture references
description:
Central pure-function resolver that both the TUI preview and `himitsu codegen <env>` depend on. Given the full env map + a target label, return the concrete resolved entries (key → secret path, no wildcards, no captures remaining).

## Behavior

- Input: `&Config.envs`, target label (e.g. `foo/*` or `foo/dev`).
- Resolution:
  1. If label is concrete, return its entries verbatim (Aliases already resolved to path refs).
  2. If label ends in `/*`, enumerate sibling envs whose labels match `foo/<segment>` (or deeper — TBD in schema design), and for each child, substitute captures into the glob's entries (e.g. `\$1` → the matched segment).
  3. Merge overlapping keys: concrete child entries win over wildcard-inherited ones.
- Output: `Vec<ResolvedEntry { key: String, secret_path: String }>` plus diagnostics for unresolved captures or missing targets.

## Why a dedicated resolver

Codegen (hm-nm9) and the TUI preview (hm-kli) both need identical semantics. Putting it in one place means users never see a mismatch between TUI preview and generated SOPS output.

## Acceptance

- Table-driven tests for: concrete label, wildcard with 0/1/N children, nested wildcards (if allowed by schema), captures in Alias paths, capture index out of range.
- Resolver returns deterministic order (sort by key) so generated SOPS files are diff-friendly.
notes:
## Output shape correction (2026-04-21)

The resolver returns a **nested tree**, not a flat `Vec<ResolvedEntry>`. Shape (proposed):

```rust
enum EnvNode {
    Leaf { secret_path: String },
    Branch(BTreeMap<String, EnvNode>),
}
```

Reasoning: `foo/*` expansion over children `foo/bar` and `foo/bar/baz` must produce a single nested structure, not a flat list and not multiple resolver invocations. Captures (`\$1`) still substitute path segments, but the output is always a tree rooted at the queried label.

Glob entries reference secret variables only — never other env groups. The resolver does not follow cross-env references.

Determinism: `BTreeMap` gives sorted keys → diff-friendly downstream codegen.
close_reason: Wave 2 landed in commit. Resolver returns deterministic EnvNode trees for concrete + wildcard labels. Cache mirrors envs into SQLite with scope isolation, SHA-256 staleness, atomic refresh. 18 new tests green.

title: TUI Envs tab: list, create, edit, delete presets
description:
Add a dedicated Envs tab alongside the existing TUI views (see rust/src/tui/views/). It becomes the primary UX for authoring preset environments.

## UX sketch

- Left pane: tree of env labels grouped by scope (project first, then global). `foo`, `foo/bar`, `foo/*` render as a hierarchy.
- Right pane: the selected env's resolved entries — Singles, Aliases, and the expanded form of any Glob (so users see what `foo/*` actually produces).
- Keymap reuses the project's existing conventions (rust/src/tui/keymap.rs): `n` new env, `e` edit, `d` delete, `/` search, `Enter` drill in.
- Inline editor for entry kinds (Single | Glob | Alias) with a live preview column showing capture-group expansions.

## Wiring

- Reads come from the SQLite cache (hm-8ht).
- Writes go through the mutation layer (hm-d1m); optimistic UI with toast rollback on failure.
- Scope indicator visible at all times; creation defaults to project scope inside a git repo, global otherwise.

## Acceptance

- Can create `foo`, `foo/bar`, and `foo/*` entries end-to-end from the TUI.
- Editing an Alias with a capture ref renders the live expansion for each matched child env.
- Deleting an env removes it from YAML + cache on disk.
notes:
## Scope reduction (2026-04-21)

**First slice: read-only + delete.** Creation and inline editing are deferred to follow-up issues (to be filed when this one lands).

Included in v1:
- Tree/list view of envs grouped by scope (project/global).
- Right pane shows resolved entries (Singles, Aliases, expanded Globs via hm-20e).
- `d` deletes the selected env (with confirmation), routed through the mutation API (hm-d1m).
- `/` search, scope indicator, navigation.

Deferred to follow-ups:
- `n` new env flow with inline editor.
- `e` edit existing entries.
- Live capture-reference preview editor.

Rationale: smallest slice that validates the cache + resolver + mutation stack end-to-end through real UI. Creation UX deserves its own design pass once read-path is proven.
close_reason: Read+delete TUI tab landed. Epic complete: schema + cache + resolver + mutation + codegen sops + TUI all merged. 5 new view tests; 337 lib tests pass.

title: Config mutation layer: write envs to project or global scope
description:
Add a mutation API that the TUI and any future CLI subcommands call to create/update/delete env presets. Scope is inferred the same way `codegen` already does it (rust/src/cli/codegen.rs:118-127): walk up for `.himitsu.yaml`, else fall back to global config.

## Requirements

- Single entry point: `envs::upsert(label, entries, scope_hint) -> Result<()>`; sibling `envs::delete(label, scope)`.
- Preserve YAML formatting/comments where feasible (serde_yaml round-trip is lossy — evaluate `yaml-rust2` or string-level edits if comments matter here).
- Atomic writes: temp file + rename so a crash mid-write does not corrupt config.
- After every mutation, refresh the SQLite cache row for that scope.
- Validation: reject labels that violate the grammar from the schema-design task.

## Acceptance

- Unit tests for project vs global routing, label validation, atomic write behavior.
- Integration test: mutate via API → reload `Config` from disk → observe change.
notes:
## YAML fidelity decision (2026-04-21)

**Accept lossy round-trip via `serde_yaml`.** Comments and custom formatting in `.himitsu.yaml` / global config will not survive a mutation. This matches the current codebase pattern and keeps the implementation small.

- Document the lossy behavior in the TUI help panel + user-facing docs.
- Atomic write (temp file + rename) still required to avoid partial writes.
close_reason: Landed: upsert/delete/read API with ScopeHint inference, atomic writes, validation, cache refresh. Config gained first-class envs field. 11 new tests green.

title: SQLite cache for env presets (project + global scope)
description:
Mirror `envs:` from YAML config into SQLite so the TUI can list/search without re-parsing YAML on every keystroke, and so other subsystems (codegen, check) have a uniform read API.

## Requirements

- Tables (proposed):
  - `envs(id INTEGER PK, label TEXT, scope TEXT CHECK(scope IN ('project','global')), config_path TEXT, mtime INTEGER)`
  - `env_entries(id INTEGER PK, env_id INTEGER REFERENCES envs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, kind TEXT, value TEXT, alias_key TEXT)`
- Rebuild strategy: on load, compare config file hash/mtime against cached row; rebuild if stale.
- Scope handling: project rows keyed by git-root path so switching projects does not pollute.
- YAML remains source of truth — every write path must rewrite YAML then refresh the cache; never the reverse.

## Non-goals

- No schema migrations framework yet — initial table ships with the feature.
- No cross-device sync; the cache is purely local.

## Acceptance

- Round-trip test: load YAML → cache → query → matches parsed `Config.envs`.
- Stale detection: mutating YAML on disk triggers cache rebuild on next read.
close_reason: Wave 2 landed in commit. Resolver returns deterministic EnvNode trees for concrete + wildcard labels. Cache mirrors envs into SQLite with scope isolation, SHA-256 staleness, atomic refresh. 18 new tests green.

title: Design env schema: wildcard semantics + capture references
description:
Formalize the env label/entry model before any TUI or codegen work lands. The current `EnvEntry` enum (rust/src/config/mod.rs:134) has `Single`, `Glob`, `Alias` — decide how capture references like `/\$1/postgres-url` are expressed on-disk and in-memory.

## Questions to resolve

- Label grammar: confirm `foo`, `foo/bar`, `foo/*` are the only legal forms; reject `foo/*/bar`? Or allow mid-path wildcards?
- Does a `foo/*` env enumerate its children by scanning other env labels in config, or by scanning the secret namespace? (Probably: scan sibling env labels that match `foo/<something>`.)
- Capture-ref syntax: `\$1`, `\${1}`, `{{1}}`? Which path segments are captured — everything matched by `*`?
- Do captures only make sense inside `Glob` entries, or also inside `Alias.path`? Likely the latter: `Alias { key: DB_URL, path: \"/\$1/postgres-url\" }` under a `foo/*` env.
- Round-trip serde: new `EnvEntry` variant or reuse existing `Glob`/`Alias` with pattern detection?

## Deliverable

A short design doc (in this issue's `--design`) + updated serde tests in rust/src/config/mod.rs covering:
- Capture-ref in Alias paths
- Invalid label rejection
- Expansion semantics (input env set → resolved concrete entries)
notes:
## Locked constraints (2026-04-21)

- **Reject mid-path wildcards.** Only `foo`, `foo/bar`, `foo/*` are valid. `foo/*/bar`, `*/foo`, and similar are hard errors at config-parse time.
- **Glob entries reference secret variables only, never other env groups.** Inside a `foo/*` env, entries describe secret paths (optionally with `\$1` captures) — they do not pull in other env labels.
- **Wildcards produce nested output, never multiple files.** `foo/*` expanded over concrete children `foo/bar`, `foo/bar/baz` yields a single tree:
  ```
  foo:
    bar:
      baz: <value>
  ```
  This mirrors how sops would naturally represent a hierarchical namespace.

These constraints must be enforced by the label grammar validator and reflected in the resolver's output shape (which is a tree, not a flat list — see hm-20e).
close_reason: Schema + validator landed: validate_env_label, parse_captures, validate_envs, ProjectConfig::validate. 15 new tests green, full workspace builds clean. Mid-path wildcards, bare-wildcard, bad chars, capture-in-concrete-env, and high-capture-index all rejected. Resolver (hm-20e) and codegen (hm-ic2) can now consume these helpers.

title: Preset environment management (TUI tab + codegen integration)
description:
## Motivation

Users already declare environment presets in config under `envs:` as a `BTreeMap<String, Vec<EnvEntry>>` with `Single`, `Glob`, and `Alias` variants (see rust/src/config/mod.rs:116, :134). Today this config is only consumable by read paths — there is no UX for authoring, visualizing, or sharing envs, and `himitsu codegen` currently emits language bindings rather than deployable secret bundles.

This epic adds a first-class preset environment manager that:
- Surfaces envs as a dedicated tab in the TUI
- Treats env labels as a path namespace: `foo`, `foo/bar`, `foo/*` are all valid, where `foo/*` is a wildcard shortcut that expands across children
- Supports capture-group references inside wildcard entries, e.g. `/$1/postgres-url` lets `foo/*` expand into `foo/dev/postgres-url`, `foo/prod/postgres-url`, etc.
- Mutates the correct config file based on context (project `.himitsu.yaml` vs global config)
- Mirrors the config into the SQLite cache so reads are fast and the TUI stays responsive
- Extends `himitsu codegen <env>` to emit `<env>.sops.yaml` — a valid SOPS-encrypted secret bundle — so envs become deployable artifacts, not just labels

## Data model

Conceptually this is a many-to-many relationship: envs ↔ secrets. In SQLite this is cached as (example, subject to design child):

- `envs(id, label, scope)` — `scope` ∈ {project, global}; `label` stores the full namespaced path (`foo`, `foo/bar`)
- `env_entries(env_id, kind, value, alias_key, capture_pattern)` — one row per EnvEntry, preserving Single/Glob/Alias semantics

Authoritative storage stays YAML; SQLite is a rebuilt cache keyed off config file mtime/hash.

## Scope boundary

- Scope (project vs global) is inferred the same way `codegen` already does it — walk up for `.himitsu.yaml`, else global.
- All mutations are writes to the YAML config plus a cache-refresh; no new storage of truth in SQLite.

## Out of scope

- Changing how secrets themselves are stored/encrypted
- Re-keying or rotation flows (separate surface)
- Any non-SOPS codegen output format (typescript/go/etc. stay as-is)

## Success criteria

- TUI has an Envs tab that lists, creates, edits, and deletes env presets with wildcard + alias support
- Wildcard capture references (`$1`, `$2`, ...) resolve correctly in both TUI preview and codegen
- `himitsu codegen <env>` produces a `<env>.sops.yaml` that round-trips through `sops` without manual editing
- SQLite cache stays consistent with YAML after every mutation; restart reflects on-disk state
close_reason: Read+delete TUI tab landed. Epic complete: schema + cache + resolver + mutation + codegen sops + TUI all merged. 5 new view tests; 337 lib tests pass.

Cli::launch_tui() in rust/src/cli/mod.rs calls tui::run() directly instead of spawning himitsu-tui. Terminal state restored on panic via drop guard or set_hook.

title: Replace OpenTUI TUI with Rust/ratatui
description: Replace the Bun + @opentui/core TUI (in tui/, launched as a subprocess via himitsu-tui) with a pure-Rust TUI built on ratatui + crossterm, living inside the existing himitsu binary at rust/src/tui/. Running 'himitsu' with no subcommand will launch the new TUI in-process, eliminating the subprocess hop, the HIMITSU_BIN fallback, and the entire Bun toolchain from the build. Full PRD in commit history / conversation.

title: TUI envs tab: edit existing env with live capture preview
description:
Follow-up to hm-zzj. v1 ships read + delete; this adds `e` to edit an existing env's entries.

## UX

- `e` opens the same editor as the create flow (file separately as hm-zzj-followup-create) prefilled with the selected env's current entries.
- Right pane continues to show the live `env_resolver::resolve` output as the user edits, so capture-ref expansions (`/$1/postgres-url` against actual store secrets) update in real time.
- Save calls `envs_mut::upsert` (which is upsert semantics — replaces existing rows).

## Notes

- Depends on the create flow (shares the editor widget). Build the editor once, reuse it.
- For wildcard envs, the live preview must enumerate `available_secrets` via `crate::remote::store::list_secrets` to compute capture matches.
close_reason: Added env edit flow with prefilled editor, Ctrl-S upsert save, live preview while editing, and single-entry edit tests; filed hm-cpf for multi-entry editing

title: TUI envs tab: create new env flow
description:
Follow-up to hm-zzj. v1 of the envs tab shipped read + delete only. This adds the `n` flow to create a new env from inside the tab.

## UX

- `n` opens an inline editor split with two fields:
  1. Label input — validated live against `validate_env_label`. Show grammar hints (concrete vs `<prefix>/*`).
  2. Entries list — repeatable rows, each picking a kind (Single | Glob | Alias) and the path/key fields.
- Save (`Ctrl+S` or `Enter` on a "Save" button) calls `envs_mut::upsert(label, entries, scope_hint, cwd)`; on success, reload and select the new label. On error, toast the message and keep the editor open.
- `Esc` cancels with a confirmation if the form is dirty.

## Wiring

- Route through the same `View::Envs` slot or add a sub-modal — your call when implementing.
- Reuse the keymap binding `keymap.envs` already defined; add a sub-binding under it for `n`.
close_reason: Added TUI envs new-env flow with inline editor, live validation, save via envs_mut::upsert, toasts, and tests
